latest.title: Net Asset Value(s)
latest.content:
7 Apr 2026 11:39
RNS Number : 4889Z
JPMorgan Emerging EMEA Securities
07 April 2026
JPMORGAN EMERGING EUROPE, MIDDLE EAST & AFRICA SECURITIES PLC (the 'Company')
Legal Entity Identifier: 549300II3MHI98ZLVH37
THE COMPANY ANNOUNCES THE UNAUDITED NET ASSET VALUE (NAV) As at: 06 April 2026
THE NET ASSET VALUE PER SHARE IN PENCE, INCLUDING INCOME WITH DEBT AT FAIR VALUE:
66.53
The Directors consider that in the absence of observable market data on its Russian investments resulting from the closure of the Moscow Exchange (MOEX) to overseas investors, there has been a material change to the market value of its Russian investments and therefore a fair value valuation methodology has been applied to those investments held. For its MOEX local stock, an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 25th February 2022. The price of these stocks has been determined by taking the live market price as at 25th February 2022 and applying a 99% provision for valuation. Similarly, for the American Depositary Receipts and Global Depositary Receipts an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 2nd March 2022 and a 99% provision for valuation applied.
Name of contact and telephone number for queries:
Paul Ainger 0044 207 742 6524
Name of authorised company official responsible for making this notification:
Paul Winship 0044 207 742 9815 - Company Secretary
Date: 07 April 2026

company_30s.what_they_do: JEMA Ltd is a UK‑registered investment vehicle that holds a portfolio of assets, many of which are tied to international sanctions regimes. It manages those holdings, pays dividends, and publishes net asset value (NAV) updates for shareholders.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the crossroads of geopolitical risk and investment opportunity. By holding assets that could become valuable if sanctions are lifted, JEMA offers a way for investors to gain exposure to a niche market that is largely ignored by mainstream funds.
company_30s.current_state: JEMA is in a low‑price, low‑volume phase, but it has just released a series of updates – a NAV report, AGM results, court‑case status, and a list of its largest investments. The stock trades at a modest price that is well below the NAV figure reported, suggesting it is currently undervalued relative to the underlying asset base.

rns_narrative: The company is keeping the market informed about its financial health by issuing regular NAV updates and AGM results, which reassure investors that the asset base is stable.
Legal proceedings are underway, and JEMA is actively managing court‑case outcomes that could affect its holdings and shareholder value.
A focus on major investments – both acquisitions and divestitures – shows that JEMA is looking to strengthen its portfolio and improve liquidity.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but watching for a catalyst
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders see the current low price as a buying opportunity, especially if sanctions ease or a court ruling goes in JEMA’s favour.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others warn that ongoing sanctions and legal uncertainty could keep the stock stuck at low levels, and the lack of liquidity may trap holders.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has stayed largely silent, but bullish chatter has edged up after the AGM, suggesting a possible shift toward optimism if a positive development arrives.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, which means the market is not paying attention – the perfect set‑up for a surprise catalyst to move the stock.

near_term_catalysts: A favourable court ruling that removes restrictions on a key asset could lift the NAV and trigger a price rally.
A public announcement of a new investment or divestiture that improves liquidity and signals stronger fundamentals.
Any news that sanctions are being relaxed or a new trade agreement is signed, which would instantly increase the value of JEMA’s holdings.
A sudden spike in trading volume – especially if institutional buyers step in – that could confirm the end of capitulation.
A technical breakout above the recent low with sustained volume, indicating the start of a recovery.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on the next NAV release, court‑case updates, and any mention of sanctions in the news. A volume spike or a reversal of the 52‑week low would be the clearest confirmation of a bounce.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a potential recovery. A tight price compression suggests that a small catalyst could trigger a large move, but the absence of a clear signal means the market is still waiting for that trigger.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: JEMA’s current price is far below the NAV figure reported, and the company’s legal and investment updates show it is actively working to improve its asset base. If a court ruling clears a key holding or sanctions are eased, the stock could rebound 200% or more – a classic mean‑reversion scenario after a deep crash.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that sanctions and legal challenges persist, keeping the NAV locked and the price stuck. A worst‑case scenario is a further slide to the low‑single‑digit pound range, which would wipe out most of the current equity.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~200% (from ~£7.18 to ~£21.54). Potential downside: ~70% (down to ~£2.15). The risk‑reward ratio is roughly 3:1, making it an asymmetric play that rewards patience.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the recent low with a volume spike of at least 1.5× the average. Allocate 5–10% of your portfolio to this position, set a stop 20% below entry, and be prepared to hold for several months as the mean‑reversion plays out.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a short position until you see one of the catalysts listed above – a favourable court ruling, a new investment announcement, or a clear signal of sanctions easing. A sudden volume surge or a technical breakout would trigger a quick entry.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there is no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risk.

ai_insights.bottom_line: JEMA’s deep crash and dead market attention set the stage for a potentially explosive rebound once a catalyst – such as a favourable court ruling or sanctions easing – hits, offering a high upside with limited downside if you enter early.
